summary: Remote code execution on require via jsonkeeper.com paste fetch
details: On module load, index.js unconditionally invokes an initPlugin() routine that HTTP-GETs https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/JTOGJ, parses the JSON response, and executes the `cookie` field as JavaScript via `new Function('require', <payload>)(require)`. This gives the paste owner arbitrary code execution inside any Node.js process that require()s the package, with the full `require` function bound into the executed code. The paste content is mutable and attacker-controlled, so today's payload can be swapped for any follow-on action (credential theft, backdoor install, further droppers) without republishing the package. The package's declared purpose ("lightweight routing framework built on top of Express.js") has no legitimate need for outbound HTTP on import, and the name mimics common Express routing helpers — a lure combined with a remote-eval dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
